problems.title: Vectorized Addition
problems.description: Add 5 to each element of the array [1, 2, 3]. Expected output: [6 7 8]. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Add 5 to each element of the array [1, 2, 3]. Expected output: [6 7 8]
problems.starter_code:
from numpy import *

def main(input_1, value):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = [1, 2, 3]

    value = 5
    main(input_1, value)
problems.solution:
from numpy import *

def main(input_1, value):
    arr = array(input_1) + value
    print(arr)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = [1, 2, 3]

    value = 5
    main(input_1, value)

problems.title: Array Mathematical Operations
problems.description: Find the sine of each element in the array [π/2, π, 3π/2]. Expected output: [1. 0. -1.]. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Find the sine of each element in the array [π/2, π, 3π/2]. Expected output: [1. 0. -1.]
problems.starter_code:
from numpy import *

def main(value, value_2, value_3):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    value = 2

    value_2 = 3

    value_3 = 2
    main(value, value_2, value_3)
problems.solution:
from numpy import *

def main(value, value_2, value_3):
    arr = sin(array([pi / value, pi, value_2 * pi / value_3]))
    print(arr)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    value = 2

    value_2 = 3

    value_3 = 2
    main(value, value_2, value_3)

problems.title: Sum of Array Elements
problems.description: Calculate the sum of the array [1, 2, 3, 4]. Expected output: 10. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Calculate the sum of the array [1, 2, 3, 4]. Expected output: 10
problems.starter_code:
from numpy import *

def main(input_1):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = [1, 2, 3, 4]
    main(input_1)
problems.solution:
from numpy import *

def main(input_1):
    arr = sum(array(input_1))
    print(arr)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = [1, 2, 3, 4]
    main(input_1)

problems.title: Array Concatenation
problems.description: Concatenate arrays [1, 2] and [3, 4]. Expected output: [1 2 3 4]. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.starter_code:
from numpy import *

def main(input_1, input_2):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = [1, 2]

    input_2 = [3, 4]
    main(input_1, input_2)
problems.solution:
from numpy import *

def main(input_1, input_2):
    arr = concatenate((array(input_1), array(input_2)))
    print(arr)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_1 = [1, 2]

    input_2 = [3, 4]
    main(input_1, input_2)

problems.title: Aliasing Arrays
problems.description: Create an alias for the array [1, 2, 3] and change the first element in the alias. Expected output: Original: [10 2 3], Alias: [10 2 3]. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create an alias for the array [1, 2, 3] and change the first element in the alias. Expected output: Original: [10 2 3], Alias: [10 2 3]
problems.starter_code:
from numpy import *

def main(arr, arr_alias):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    arr = array([1, 2, 3])

    arr_alias = arr
    main(arr, arr_alias)
problems.solution:
from numpy import *

def main(arr, arr_alias):
    arr_alias[0] = 10
    print(arr, 'Alias:', arr_alias)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    arr = array([1, 2, 3])

    arr_alias = arr
    main(arr, arr_alias)

problems.title: Shallow Copy
problems.description: Create a shallow copy of [1, 2, 3] using 'view()' and modify the first element of the copy. Expected output: Original: [10 2 3], Copy: [10 2 3]. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create a shallow copy of [1, 2, 3] using 'view()' and modify the first element of the copy. Expected output: Original: [10 2 3], Copy: [10 2 3]
problems.starter_code:
from numpy import *

def main(arr1, arr2):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    arr1 = array([1, 2, 3])

    arr2 = arr1.view()
    main(arr1, arr2)
problems.solution:
from numpy import *

def main(arr1, arr2):
    arr2[0] = 10
    print(arr1, 'Copy:', arr2)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    arr1 = array([1, 2, 3])

    arr2 = arr1.view()
    main(arr1, arr2)

problems.title: Deep Copy
problems.description: Create a deep copy of [1, 2, 3] using 'copy()' and modify the first element of the copy. Expected output: Original: [1 2 3], Copy: [10 2 3]. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create a deep copy of [1, 2, 3] using 'copy()' and modify the first element of the copy. Expected output: Original: [1 2 3], Copy: [10 2 3]
problems.starter_code:
from numpy import *

def main(arr3, arr4):
    # Write your solution here.
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    arr3 = array([1, 2, 3])

    arr4 = arr3.copy()
    main(arr3, arr4)
problems.solution:
from numpy import *

def main(arr3, arr4):
    arr4[0] = 10
    print(arr3, 'Copy:', arr4)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    arr3 = array([1, 2, 3])

    arr4 = arr3.copy()
    main(arr3, arr4)
